title: Review internal/enrichers against ENR-* requirements
description: Review all enrichers in internal/enrichers/ against AGENTS.md ENR-* requirements. Check: Fanart.tv (ENR-FA-001 to ENR-FA-005), Last.fm (ENR-LF-001 to ENR-LF-007), MusicBrainz (ENR-MB-001 to ENR-MB-007), OpenAI (ENR-AI-001 to ENR-AI-007), rate limiting, caching, error handling.

title: Auth entities missing created_at timestamps
description:
**Files**: 
- ent/schema/spotifyauth.go
- ent/schema/navidromeauth.go  
- ent/schema/lastfmauth.go

**Issue**: Auth entities (SpotifyAuth, NavidromeAuth, LastFMAuth) are missing created_at timestamp fields that are present on nearly all other entities in the schema.

**Impact**:
- Inconsistent schema design
- Harder to debug when auth records were created
- Missing audit trail for security/debugging purposes

**Action Required**:
1. Add created_at field to SpotifyAuth schema
2. Add created_at field to NavidromeAuth schema
3. Add created_at field to LastFMAuth schema
4. Follow the pattern from other schemas:
   
5. Generate migration for existing records (set to current time or last_synced_at)

title: Review internal/views against VIEW-* requirements
description: Review all view templates in internal/views/ against AGENTS.md VIEW-* requirements. Check: sync status badges (VIEW-SS-001 to VIEW-SS-009), track thumbnails (VIEW-TT-001 to VIEW-TT-004), progress indicators, match statistics display, error message presentation.

title: Review internal/vibes against VIB-* requirements
description: Review AI mixtape generation in internal/vibes/ against AGENTS.md VIB-* requirements. Check: mixtape generation (VIB-GEN-001 to VIB-GEN-007), playlist enhancement (VIB-ENH-001 to VIB-ENH-005), JSON parsing (VIB-PAR-001 to VIB-PAR-006), track matching, fuzzy matching, error handling.

title: Audit external API usage for rate limiting and etiquette
description: Audit all external API usage against AGENTS.md external API etiquette standards. Check: 429 response handling, exponential backoff, batch API usage (e.g., Spotify Audio Features), descriptive User-Agent strings, request throttling, caching strategies.

title: Review internal/config against CFG-* requirements
description: Review configuration in internal/config/ against AGENTS.md CFG-* requirements. Check: required field validation (CFG-001 to CFG-005), default values (CFG-006), environment variable overrides (CFG-007), YAML loading (CFG-008), sensitive data handling.

title: Review error handling across codebase against ERR-* requirements
description: Review error handling patterns across the entire codebase against AGENTS.md ERR-* requirements. Check: HTTP status codes (ERR-001), descriptive error messages (ERR-002), network timeouts (ERR-003), external API failures (ERR-004), error logging (ERR-005), database failures (ERR-006), retry logic (ERR-007), no sensitive data in errors (ERR-008), JSON parsing errors (ERR-009), rate limit handling (ERR-010).

title: String length validation missing on schema fields
description: Per VAL-006, the system SHOULD validate string lengths to prevent buffer overflows. Currently, string fields in schemas (e.g., name, description, bio) have no MaxLen() validation. This could lead to database errors or potential security issues. Should add reasonable max length constraints to string fields across all schemas.

title: Review internal/handlers against HAND-* requirements
description: Review all HTTP handlers in internal/handlers/ against AGENTS.md HAND-* requirements. Check: user isolation (HAND-AL-002, HAND-PL-002), sync status display (HAND-PL-003, HAND-PL-004), match statistics (HAND-PL-005), enrichment triggers (HAND-AL-003), mixtape validation (HAND-VB-002, HAND-VB-003), error handling.

title: Review internal/database for error handling and context usage
description: Review database layer in internal/database/ against AGENTS.md coding standards. Check: context.Context usage, error wrapping with fmt.Errorf, structured logging with slog, connection failure handling (ERR-006), transaction management, query optimization.

title: PlaylistTrack missing direct user edge for efficient isolation
description: PlaylistTrack entity doesn't have a direct user edge, relying on traversal through Playlist->User for user isolation. Per VAL-003 and HAND-PL-002, user ownership validation is required. While indirect traversal works, a direct user edge would improve query performance and simplify user isolation enforcement. Consider adding edge.From('user', User.Type) to PlaylistTrack schema.

title: Context usage violation in async goroutines
description: Multiple handlers use r.Context() in background goroutines which will be cancelled when HTTP response completes. Examples: vibes.go:624 (GenerateMixtape), albums.go:567 (AlbumCreateMixtape), playlists.go:902 (EnhanceVibes). This violates Go context best practices and could cause operations to fail prematurely. Should use context.Background() or context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), ...) for async operations.

title: SECURITY: NavidromeAuth stores passwords in plaintext
description:
**File**: ent/schema/navidromeauth.go:19

**Issue**: The NavidromeAuth schema stores the Navidrome password in plaintext. There's a comment acknowledging this should be encrypted at rest, but encryption is not implemented.

**Violation**: ERR-008 - The system MUST NOT expose sensitive information (API keys, passwords) in error messages. While this is about storage not error messages, storing plaintext passwords violates security best practices.

**Requirement**: AUTH-008 - The system MUST NOT expose Navidrome credentials in responses.

**Action Required**:
1. Implement at-rest encryption for the password field
2. Use a secure encryption method (AES-256 or similar)
3. Ensure decryption happens only when needed for authentication
4. Consider using application-level encryption hooks in Ent

title: AlbumRecommendation struct should be moved to separate package
description:
**File**: ent/schema/album.go:12-20

**Issue**: The AlbumRecommendation struct is defined directly in the schema file, which is unusual for Ent schemas. Struct types used in JSON fields are typically defined in a separate package for better organization and reusability.

**Current Code**:
Lines 12-20 define AlbumRecommendation struct in the schema file

**Action Required**:
1. Move AlbumRecommendation to internal/models or similar package
2. Import and reference it in the album schema
3. This improves code organization and makes the struct reusable

**Priority**: Low - This is a code organization issue, not a functional bug

title: Review internal/events for error handling and reliability
description: Review event system in internal/events/ against AGENTS.md coding standards. Check: error handling, context usage, event delivery guarantees, SSE implementation, connection management, graceful shutdown.

title: Review cmd/server for initialization and error handling
description: Review main application in cmd/server/ against AGENTS.md coding standards. Check: configuration loading, graceful shutdown, signal handling, context cancellation, error logging, startup validation, dependency injection.

title: AlbumMixtapeModal missing user isolation check
description: Handler internal/handlers/albums.go:642 AlbumMixtapeModal() fetches album without user isolation check. It only calls h.Client.Album.Get(r.Context(), albumID) without verifying album.HasUserWith(user.ID(u.ID)). This violates HAND-AL-002 which requires user isolation. User could potentially access another user's album modal by guessing album IDs.

title: Review internal/services against SRV-* requirements
description: Review all services in internal/services/ against AGENTS.md SRV-* requirements. Check: AI enrichment logic (SRV-AI-001 to SRV-AI-006), playlist sync (SRV-PS-001 to SRV-PS-009), track matching (SRV-TM-001 to SRV-TM-010), similar artists (SRV-SA-001 to SRV-SA-004), catalog sync (SRV-SY-001 to SRV-SY-007).

title: Hardcoded salt in Subsonic authentication
description: Handler internal/handlers/auth.go:143 uses hardcoded salt 'spotter' for Subsonic API authentication. Comment says 'In production, generate random' but this is not implemented. While Subsonic protocol requires salt to be sent with each request (not stored), using a static salt reduces security. Should generate random salt per request as per AUTH-010 best practices.

title: Missing Secure flag on session cookie for HTTPS
description: Handler internal/handlers/auth.go:112-119 sets session cookie without Secure flag. The cookie is HttpOnly and SameSite=Lax, but missing Secure means cookie could be sent over HTTP. Should set Secure: true when TLS is available (check r.TLS != nil like spotify_auth.go:59 does).

title: Review internal/providers against PROV-* requirements
description: Review all providers in internal/providers/ against AGENTS.md PROV-* requirements. Check: Spotify token refresh (PROV-SP-001 to PROV-SP-005), Navidrome operations (PROV-ND-001 to PROV-ND-007), Last.fm integration (PROV-LF-001 to PROV-LF-006), error handling, pagination, token caching.

title: Review internal/types for validation requirements
description: Review type definitions in internal/types/ against AGENTS.md VAL-* requirements. Check: required field validation (VAL-001), string length validation (VAL-006), MusicBrainz ID format validation (VAL-007), confidence threshold validation (VAL-004), timestamp format validation (VAL-005).

title: Review test coverage against AGENTS.md testing standards
description: Review test coverage across codebase against AGENTS.md testing standards. Check: external API mocking with httptest.NewServer, no real network calls, coverage of happy paths, auth errors (401/403), rate limits (429), 404 handling, test organization, table-driven tests.

title: MusicBrainz ID format validation missing
description:
**Files**: Multiple schema files with musicbrainz_id fields

**Issue**: VAL-007 in AGENTS.md states: "The system MUST validate that MusicBrainz IDs are in correct UUID format". 

Currently, musicbrainz_id fields are defined as simple strings without UUID format validation:
- ent/schema/artist.go:27
- ent/schema/album.go:36  
- ent/schema/track.go:23

**Action Required**:
Option 1 - Add Ent validators to schema files
Option 2 - Add application-level validation before database writes
Option 3 - Use a UUID field type instead of String

**Recommendation**: Add Match() validator to ensure UUID format at schema level.

title: Review integration layer against INT-* requirements
description: Review integration implementations against AGENTS.md INT-* requirements. Check: Navidrome integration (INT-001), optional integrations (INT-002 to INT-006), OpenAI integration (INT-007), graceful degradation (INT-008, INT-009), per-user configuration (INT-010).

title: Review internal/middleware against AUTH-* requirements
description: Review middleware components in internal/middleware/ against AGENTS.md AUTH-* requirements. Check: session validation, authentication enforcement, credential protection (AUTH-008), cookie security (AUTH-005), user context propagation, error handling for auth failures.

title: SECURITY: OAuth tokens and session keys stored in plaintext
description: SpotifyAuth stores access_token and refresh_token in plaintext. LastFMAuth stores session_key in plaintext. While less critical than passwords, these authentication tokens grant access to user accounts and should be encrypted at rest per security best practices. See AUTH-008 requirement.

title: Track entity missing user edge for isolation
description:
**File**: ent/schema/track.go

**Issue**: The Track schema doesn't have a direct edge to User, unlike Artist and Album entities. This makes user isolation queries more complex and could lead to data leakage if not handled carefully.

**Violation**: Affects enforcement of AUTH-002 and HAND-AL-002 (user isolation requirements).

**Current Structure**:
- Artist has user edge ✓
- Album has user edge ✓  
- Track has NO user edge ✗

**Action Required**:
1. Add a user edge to Track schema similar to Artist and Album
2. Update Track creation code to set user relationship
3. Update queries to leverage the direct user edge
4. Consider migration path for existing Track records

**Alternative**: Document why tracks don't need user edges (e.g., tracks belong to albums which belong to users, so user isolation is implicit through traversal).

title: Review code in ent/ against the instructions in AGETNS.md and file tickets for anything you find.
close_reason: Completed comprehensive review of ent/ schemas against AGENTS.md requirements. Found and filed 3 new issues: spotter-vey (OAuth tokens in plaintext), spotter-5rf (string length validation), spotter-909 (PlaylistTrack user edge). Confirmed 5 existing issues are valid: spotter-ahw (password plaintext), spotter-wat (Track user edge), spotter-r2a (MusicBrainz validation), spotter-06e (auth timestamps), spotter-c3i (AlbumRecommendation struct).
